text: Although numerous attempts have been made, MOFs based on alkali metals with permanent porosity are rarely reported owing to the strong coordination interaction of alkali metals with solvent molecules, such as water. Nevertheless, MOFs based on alkali metals often possess a gravimetric benefit for gas adsorption due to their low density. Herein, a sodium-based microporous MOF, namely HIAM-111, composed of an octacarboxylate ligand and 8-connected Na₄ clusters is prepared. It possesses a 3²,4²,8-c unprecedented 3D network topology with excellent structural stability, which affords a BET surface area of 1561 m²/g, a value far larger than that of all reported Na-MOFs. Additional experiments show that HIAM-111 is favorable for separation of the C₂H₂/CO₂ mixture and can efficiently purify the ternary mixture of C₂H₄/C₂H₂/C₂H₆ by a single adsorption step with excellent adsorption capacities at all times in the adsorption process. Our work offers an important route for the design of alkali-metal-based stable and highly porous MOFs and the corresponding potential application in gas separation.
